items.title: American Express Sports Deals Link Stadium Spending To Valuation Debate
items.description: American Express (NYSE:AXP) has expanded its sports partnerships across major U.S. venues and professional teams. The company is now the Official Payments Partner for MetLife Stadium, Mercedes Benz Stadium, and several major league franchises. These long term agreements aim to integrate premium event access and cardholder benefits directly into live game and concert experiences. For American Express, sports and entertainment tie directly into its focus on higher spending consumers and...

items.title: Should You Forget PayPal (PYPL) and Buy American Express (AXP) Instead?
items.description: The blue chip financial giant has a brighter future than the digital payments leader.

items.title: Stablecoin firms bet big on AI agent payments that barely exist
items.description: Two weeks ago, a Citrini Research scenario imagining AI agents routing around card network fees sent Visa Inc., Mastercard Inc. and American Express Co. shares tumbling as much as 5% in a single session.  On its Feb. 25 earnings call, Circle Chief Executive Officer Jeremy Allaire argued that stablecoins could become the native currency of machine-to-machine commerce.

items.title: Berkshire CEO Greg Abel on working with Buffett, Kraft Heinz and using all his salary to buy the stock
items.description: Berkshire Hathaway's new CEO, Greg Abel, generated some significant headlines during a roughly half-hour live interview on CNBC's "Squawk Box."

items.title: New CEO Greg Abel Did Not List 2 of Berkshire Hathaway's Largest Equity Positions as "Core Holdings." Are They on the Chopping Block?
items.description: New Berkshire Hathaway CEO Greg Abel recently issued his first annual letter to shareholders, detailing his plans for Berkshire's massive equities portfolio.
